---
title: "White Moss (Leucobryum albidum)"
slug: "leucobryum-albidum"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621862361/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "White Moss"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Bryopsida"
tax_order: "Dicranales"
tax_family: "Leucobryaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Bryopsida", "Dicranales", "Leucobryaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621862361/medium.jpg" alt="White Moss" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/jimw3">
jimw3</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341581657">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Bryopsida
-   **Order:** Dicranales
-   **Family:** Leucobryaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=164650)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in shady pine flatwoods and oak hammocks.

## Natural history notes

A cushion-forming moss characterized by its pale green to whitish, rounded clumps that often resemble pincushions. In Florida, it is found in moist, shaded habitats such as forests, rotting logs, and sandy soils. It grows year-round in suitable conditions and helps retain moisture and stabilize soil in forest ecosystems. 

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/164650-Leucobryum-albidum)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/4585)
